NVDA.title: AbbVie Is Already a Dividend King. Here's Why the $10.9 Billion Apogee Deal Could Make It a Dynasty
NVDA.summary: AbbVie, though relatively young as a standalone company, has maintained its dividend growth inherited from Abbott, positioning itself as a Dividend King. The acquisition of Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion is a strategic move to bolster its drug pipeline, particularly with drugs like zumilokibart for atopic dermatitis and a pipeline in the respiratory space. This acquisition, along with its consistent revenue from products like Botox, demonstrates AbbVie's commitment to sustaining its long-term dividend growth and competing with older drug-makers.

NVDA.title: Rambus unveils DDR5 9600 RDIMM chipset for AI and data centre servers
NVDA.summary: Rambus has launched a new DDR5 9600 Server RDIMM chipset, featuring its sixth-generation Registering Clock Driver (RCD06), to meet the escalating memory demands of AI, HPC, and data center applications. This chipset enables RDIMMs to operate at speeds up to 9,600 MT/s, providing higher bandwidth and capacity crucial for large language model inference and other data-intensive workloads. The solution integrates the RCD06, PMIC5030 power management IC, and an SPD Hub with a temperature sensor, aiming to enhance signal and power integrity while simplifying design for memory module manufacturers.

NVDA.title: Nvidia Stock Hasn’t Been This Cheap Since Before 2019. How to Play NVDA Stock Here.
NVDA.summary: Nvidia's stock is currently trading at its cheapest earnings multiple since before the AI boom, despite another strong earnings report and continued leadership in the AI GPU market. While concerns about slowing China growth and increased competition exist, Wall Street analysts largely maintain "Strong Buy" ratings, projecting significant upside due to the company's expanding AI infrastructure and strong financial performance. This lower valuation presents a potential buying opportunity for investors, though long-term growth will depend on navigating challenges in key international markets.

NVDA.title: Welch & Forbes LLC Cuts Position in Bank of America Corporation $BAC
NVDA.summary: Welch & Forbes LLC reduced its stake in Bank of America Corporation by 1.4% in the first quarter, selling 13,383 shares but still owning 976,595 shares valued at $47.6 million. Despite this, Bank of America recently reported strong quarterly results, beating earnings and revenue estimates, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a target price of $62.19. The company also pays a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, and institutional investors collectively own 70.71% of the stock.

NVDA.title: MetroCity Bankshares (NASDAQ:MCBS) Lowered to "Hold" Rating by Wall Street Zen
NVDA.summary: Wall Street Zen has downgraded MetroCity Bankshares (NASDAQ:MCBS) from a "buy" to a "hold" rating, despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from other analysts with a $33.00 price target. The bank recently reported stronger-than-expected earnings of $0.77 EPS against estimates of $0.70, with revenue largely meeting expectations at $50.84 million. Institutional investors collectively hold 25.33% of the company's stock.

NVDA.title: Vantage Investment Partners LLC Purchases 74,198 Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM
NVDA.summary: Vantage Investment Partners LLC significantly increased its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) by 138.5% in the first quarter, purchasing an additional 74,198 shares. This brings their total holdings to 127,778 shares, valued at approximately $43.2 million. Other institutional investors have also increased their positions, contributing to a total institutional ownership of 16.51% of TSM stock.

NVDA.title: Procter & Gamble vs PepsiCo: The Better Stock For Passive Income Investors
NVDA.summary: This article compares the recent earnings reports and strategic directions of Procter & Gamble (P&G) and PepsiCo (PEP), discussing which might be a better investment for passive income. P&G showed broad-based growth led by its Beauty segment and is implementing cost-cutting measures, while PepsiCo experienced strong international growth but a domestic snack decline. The author leans towards P&G for its cleaner operating story and stable growth, and PepsiCo for a rebound trade given its current valuation and dividend yield.

NVDA.title: Diversify Wealth Management LLC Buys 111,142 Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM
NVDA.summary: Diversify Wealth Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) by 986% in the first quarter, adding 111,142 shares to reach a total of 122,414 shares valued at $41.37 million. This move comes as TSM reported strong earnings, beating estimates with a 40.6% year-over-year revenue increase and raising its quarterly dividend. Analyst sentiment for TSM remains positive, with an average "Buy" rating and a target price of $449.38.

MSFT.title: Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Brokerages
MSFT.summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus recommendation from 60 brokerages, with 57 buy ratings and 3 hold ratings, and an average 12-month price target of $312.79. The company recently reported strong earnings, beating EPS and revenue expectations. While investors are watching Amazon's significant AI and infrastructure spending, some analysts caution that heavy capital expenditure is impacting free cash flow and increasing debt.

MSFT.title: Travelzoo (NASDAQ:TZOO) Rating Increased to Buy at Wall Street Zen
MSFT.summary: Travelzoo (NASDAQ:TZOO) has received an upgraded rating from "hold" to "buy" by Wall Street Zen, contributing to a positive analyst outlook. The company's stock now holds a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with a price target of $15.67. This upgrade follows Travelzoo's recent earnings beat, where it reported $0.23 EPS against an estimated $0.16, though revenue slightly missed forecasts.

MSFT.title: Welch & Forbes LLC Trims Stake in O'Reilly Automotive, Inc. $ORLY
MSFT.summary: Welch & Forbes LLC recently reduced its stake in O'Reilly Automotive, Inc. by 1.3% in the first quarter, though it still holds a significant portion of shares valued at $261.4 million. This move comes amidst notable insider selling, with top executives selling over 105,000 shares for nearly $10 million in the past 90 days. Despite the insider sales, O'Reilly Automotive reported strong quarterly results, exceeding revenue and EPS expectations, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $112.25.

MSFT.title: LGT Fund Management Co Ltd. Increases Stock Position in Altria Group, Inc. $MO
MSFT.summary: LGT Fund Management Co Ltd. significantly increased its stake in Altria Group, Inc. (NYSE:MO) by 101% during the first quarter, now holding 118,495 shares valued at approximately $7.8 million. Despite mixed analyst ratings, with a consensus "Hold" and an average target price of $70.78, Altria Group reported strong quarterly results, surpassing earnings and revenue estimates. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.06 per share, offering an annualized yield of 5.9%.

MSFT.title: United Bank Buys 3,075 Shares of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
MSFT.summary: United Bank increased its stake in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) by 21% in the first quarter, adding 3,075 shares to its holdings, now totaling 17,742 shares valued at $3.61 million. AMD's stock rose 2.0% to $557.89, with a market capitalization of $909.7 billion, and the company reported strong earnings, beating analyst expectations. Despite some insider selling and ARK Invest reducing its stake, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, with several firms raising their price targets due to AMD's advancements in AI servers and data centers.

MSFT.title: Welch & Forbes LLC Cuts Position in Bank of America Corporation $BAC
MSFT.summary: Welch & Forbes LLC reduced its stake in Bank of America Corporation by 1.4% in the first quarter, selling 13,383 shares but still owning 976,595 shares valued at $47.6 million. Despite this, Bank of America recently reported strong quarterly results, beating earnings and revenue estimates, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a target price of $62.19. The company also pays a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, and institutional investors collectively own 70.71% of the stock.

MSFT.title: MetroCity Bankshares (NASDAQ:MCBS) Lowered to "Hold" Rating by Wall Street Zen
MSFT.summary: Wall Street Zen has downgraded MetroCity Bankshares (NASDAQ:MCBS) from a "buy" to a "hold" rating, despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from other analysts with a $33.00 price target. The bank recently reported stronger-than-expected earnings of $0.77 EPS against estimates of $0.70, with revenue largely meeting expectations at $50.84 million. Institutional investors collectively hold 25.33% of the company's stock.

MSFT.title: Ternium (NYSE:TX) Stock Rating Upgraded by Wall Street Zen
MSFT.summary: Wall Street Zen has upgraded Ternium (NYSE:TX) from a "buy" to a "strong-buy" rating. This upgrade aligns with a generally positive outlook from other analysts, including Goldman Sachs and Itau BBA, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average target price of $46.55. The company recently reported $1.10 EPS and $3.93 billion in quarterly revenue, with shares trading at $44.22 following the announcement.

GOOGL.title: Sam Altman Says He's ‘Not Afraid of Apple,’ Has ‘Tremendous Respect’ for iPhone Maker Amid Trade Secret Lawsuit
GOOGL.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed respect for Apple, calling it an "s-tier company," despite Apple's recent lawsuit alleging OpenAI misused trade secrets for its consumer hardware ambitions. The lawsuit claims OpenAI recruited former Apple employees, encouraged them to bypass security, and sought confidential information during interviews. This legal battle represents a significant shift from their earlier partnership and adds to OpenAI's existing legal challenges, including a copyright dispute with The New York Times.

GOOGL.title: Stock Yards Bank & Trust Co. Raises Position in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
GOOGL.summary: Stock Yards Bank & Trust Co. increased its holdings in Amazon.com, Inc. by 1.0% in the first quarter, adding 3,718 shares to own a total of 384,068 shares valued at $80 million. Despite recent insider selling, Amazon's latest quarterly results surpassed expectations with an EPS of $2.78 and revenue of $181.52 billion, leading analysts to maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating. The e-commerce giant's stock traded down 0.7% on Friday, but institutional investors own 72.20% of the company's shares.

GOOGL.title: How Wynn’s Al Marjan Island Middle East Expansion Will Impact Wynn Resorts (WYNN) Investors
GOOGL.summary: Wynn Resorts is expanding into the Middle East with its Wynn Al Marjan Island integrated resort, scheduled to open in 2027 in Ras Al Khaimah, UAE. This venture has generated significant options interest and will require balancing substantial investment with existing dividend payments. While the project could broaden Wynn's global footprint and influence future revenue, investors should consider potential risks from rising costs and leverage if the new resort underperforms.

GOOGL.title: Alphabet vs. Amazon vs. Microsoft: Which Is the Best Cloud Computing Stock to Buy Today?
GOOGL.summary: The article compares Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet as top cloud computing stocks, evaluating their performance, cloud growth, and AI infrastructure investments. It highlights Amazon's accelerating AWS revenue and profitability, Microsoft's strong Azure growth despite lagging internal AI tech, and Alphabet's top-tier AI capabilities with Gemini and TPUs. The author ultimately recommends Amazon and Alphabet, preferring Amazon for its valuation and accelerating cloud business.

GOOGL.title: Should You Buy Broadcom Inc. (AVGO)’s Shares?
GOOGL.summary: Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO) has experienced significant share price volatility this year, despite Jefferies reiterating a Buy rating due to its AI business and Google partnership. Concerns about hyperscaler spending and a failure to raise revenue guidance for its AI chips have led to a downgrade to "Hold" by Erste Group, although Carillon Eagle Growth fund still sees strong potential in Broadcom's technology within the AI space.

GOOGL.title: Radware (RDWR) Deepens AI Agent Compliance Tools But What Does That Mean For Its Moat?
GOOGL.summary: Radware recently enhanced its Agentic AI Protection solution with advanced compliance reporting and expanded protection for developer-hosted AI agents. This upgrade aligns the platform with emerging global AI standards like ISO 42001 and the EU AI Act, making it more relevant for heavily regulated enterprises. While this strengthens Radware's position in securing AI workloads and compliance, the article notes that it is more additive than transformational for its financial story, and risks like premium valuation and insider selling remain.

GOOGL.title: Alphabet C stock holds steady as Google parent leans on AI growth and ad resilience
GOOGL.summary: Alphabet Inc.'s Class C stock reflects a business significantly driven by artificial intelligence, cloud services, and robust digital advertising, with Google and YouTube serving as primary profit drivers. The article highlights how Alphabet generates most of its revenue from digital advertising while rapidly expanding its AI and cloud computing offerings, making it a key Nasdaq-listed technology group. It also details the company's strategic investments in AI and Google Cloud as future growth engines, balancing innovation with profitability, and addresses the influence of regulation, competition, and capital allocation on its stock performance.

GOOGL.title: Morgan Stanley Warns of "Chipflation" as Hyperscalers Invest More in Compute Capacity: 2 No-Brainer Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Stocks to Buy Right Now
GOOGL.summary: Morgan Stanley warns of "chipflation," a phenomenon where memory chip prices rise due to persistent demand outstripping supply from hyperscaler investments in compute capacity. Despite initial hardware deployment, the focus is shifting to utilization and returns on investment, making now a prime time to invest in companies with durable competitive advantages. The article recommends Micron Technology and Broadcom as two AI chip stocks poised to benefit from this environment due to their critical roles in memory and networking infrastructure, respectively.
